methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $98M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Type One Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Helical Fusion, a Series B nuclear energy company founded by Takaya Taguchi and Junichi Miyazawa, is evaluated against a comp universe of 3 public and 8 private peers in the nuclear energy sector, including Type One Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power. The target has raised $98M to date.

A stage-progression methodology is applicable, using the $98M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage progression. This yields an implied valuation of $186M. A peer-median approach was not viable due to insufficient narrow comparables—while 8 nuclear energy peers exist broadly, only 1 same-stage and 1 adjacent-stage peer met filtering criteria, falling short of the 3-datapoint minimum needed to avoid misleading aggregation.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $158M (low) to $214M (high), with a median of $186M. This range is derived primarily from the single applicable stage-progression method and incorporates reasonable variance bounds around that point estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct same-stage peer multiples in nuclear energy—a nascent sector with sparse public comparables—and reliance on a stage-step multiplier that may not fully capture Helical Fusion's specific technology or market position. The analysis would benefit from additional same-stage private comparable data or clarity on venture capital allocation patterns within frontier nuclear energy.
